text: Hello everyone, this is Roxen Liu with a video on how I do robotic cholecystectomy today. I've really changed the way I perform cholecystectomies with the robot. I really try to maximize the robotic platform and also recognize that there are shortcomings with the platform and try to minimize their effects. So first of all, there's no need to grab the fundus of the gallbladder to gain optimal exposure. I try to make sure that both the hook and the clipper plier or if I need to a section irrigator is in the body at the same time so I can swap between the two without having to exchange those two instruments back and forth. I like to divide the medial attachments of the gallbladder to gain a better exposure. While the clip applier is being reloaded, I make sure that the hook is moving. And the overall goal is to minimize pauses so that the operation is as efficient as possible.

The surgeon uses a clip applier to retract the liver, avoiding the use of an additional grasper for the gallbladder fundus. The clip applier is positioned to rest in the gallbladder bed, minimizing the risk of liver laceration.

The surgeon takes down remaining adhesions and begins dissection in the hepatocystic triangle using a hook. The peritoneum over the triangle is opened to expose the cystic duct and cystic artery. The cystic duct becomes visible as the medial attachments of the gallbladder are separated, allowing the gallbladder to be pulled away and the triangle to be clearly visualized.

The surgeon uses the clip applier to provide counter tension, allowing for better visualization of the cystic artery and cystic duct. The peritoneum of the gallbladder is spread to achieve the critical view of safety, and the gallbladder is partially removed for improved access.

The surgeon uses a hook to divide the cystic artery while the clip applier is reloading. The gallbladder remains partially attached to the liver, allowing for efficient removal of the remaining attachments without needing to exchange instruments, ensuring the clip applier does not bump the falciform ligament.

The surgeon activates the Firefly imaging to check for any bile leaks from the gallbladder bed. The cystic duct is divided using the cut setting of the monopolar instrument. The hook is removed, and the endocache is placed through the belly button to extract the gallbladder.
